ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cars" (
  "car_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each car model */,
  "car_name" TEXT /* Name of the car model */,
  "make" TEXT /* The manufacturer of the car */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The year the car model was produced */,
  "country" TEXT /* The country of origin */,
  "price" REAL /* The retail price of the car */,
  "color" TEXT /* The color of the car */,
  "interior_material" TEXT /* The material used for the car's interior */,
  "exterior_material" TEXT /* The material used for the car's exterior */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* The type of fuel the car uses */,
  "emissions_rating" TEXT /* The emissions rating of the car */,
  "safety_rating" TEXT /* The safety rating of the car */,
  "drivetrain" TEXT /* The type of drivetrain */,
  "mpg" REAL /* Miles per gallon (fuel efficiency) */,
  "cyl" INTEGER /* Number of cylinders */,
  "disp" REAL /* Displacement (cubic inches) */,
  "hp" INTEGER /* Horsepower */,
  "drat" REAL /* Rear axle ratio */,
  "wt" REAL /* Weight (1000 lbs) */,
  "qsec" REAL /* 1/4 mile time */,
  "vs" INTEGER /* Engine configuration (0 = V-shaped, 1 = straight) */,
  "am" INTEGER /* Transmission type (0 = automatic, 1 = manual) */,
  "gear" INTEGER /* Number of forward gears */,
  "carb" INTEGER /* Number of carburetors */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("car_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cars_am FOREIGN KEY ("am") REFERENCES transmission_types ("transmission_type"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cars_vs FOREIGN KEY ("vs") REFERENCES engine_types ("engine_config")
)

CREATE TABLE "engine_types" (
  "engine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engine configuration */,
  "engine_config" INTEGER /* Engine configuration code (0 = V-shaped, 1 = straight) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the engine configuration */,
  "max_torque" REAL /* The maximum torque of the engine */,
  "torque_at_rpm" INTEGER /* The RPM at which the maximum torque is achieved */,
  "redline_rpm" INTEGER /* The maximum RPM the engine can safely reach */,
  "engine_displacement" REAL /* The displacement of the engine in liters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transmission_types" (
  "transmission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transmission type */,
  "transmission_type" INTEGER /* Transmission type code (0 = automatic, 1 = manual) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transmission type */,
  "gear_ratios" TEXT /* A comma-separated list of gear ratios */,
  "shift_time" REAL /* The average time it takes to shift gears (seconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transmission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "car_id" INTEGER /* ID of the car model */,
  "acceleration_time" REAL /* 0-60 mph acceleration time (seconds) */,
  "top_speed" REAL /* Top speed (mph) */,
  "braking_distance" REAL /* Braking distance (feet) */,
  "fuel_tank_capacity" REAL /* Fuel tank capacity (gallons) */,
  "acceleration_0_to_100_kph" REAL /* 0-100 km/h acceleration time (seconds) */,
  "track_performance" TEXT /* Performance on a track (e.g., lap time, handling) */,
  "fuel_consumption_city" REAL /* Fuel consumption in city driving (mpg) */,
  "fuel_consumption_highway" REAL /* Fuel consumption in highway driving (mpg) */,
  "emissions" TEXT /* Emissions data (e.g., CO2, NOx) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_car_id FOREIGN KEY ("car_id") REFERENCES cars ("car_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each car model
The year the car model was produced
The retail price of the car
The material used for the car's interior
The material used for the car's exterior
The type of fuel the car uses
The emissions rating of the car
The safety rating of the car
Unique identifier for each engine configuration
The maximum torque of the engine
The RPM at which the maximum torque is achieved
The maximum RPM the engine can safely reach
The displacement of the engine in liters
Unique identifier for each transmission type
A comma-separated list of gear ratios
The average time it takes to shift gears (seconds)
Unique identifier for each performance metric entry
Performance on a track (e.g., lap time, handling)
